intro: Gandasulium horsfieldii, also known as Horsfield's jasmine, is a type of flowering plant in the jasmine family (Oleaceae) native to China and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of G. horsfieldii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, G. horsfieldii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into herbal soups and decoctions to promote relaxation and alleviate symptoms of illness.
